author: @intrinsicdrive13
text: Hello Mr Robert Murray-Smith, I was just watching your video and I wanted to leave this message specifically to thank you my good Sir for all of the fantastic and innovative information that you have shared so freely and enthusiastically. I really enjoy watching your work develop over time and cant wait to see what the future still has to hold, I hope to  one day be ordering  a good deal of super-caps in the soon to be future for my next projects. Cant wait to see what the future has to hold and I really hope they say Made in England!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thank you mate - and thank you for taking the time to write

replies.author: @intrinsicdrive13
replies.text: No problem, I'm happy to do it, least I can do to show appreciation for the great resource and learning opportunity your time has made possible for me

replies.text: bless your heart mate

author: @emailtooaj
text: @robert murray smith, may I ask what software you use to edit your videos? there may be a free plugin that could help filter out the echo/tunnel/reverb from the audio while you're rendering the final

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: openshot - i use linux mint as the OS

replies.author: @emailtooaj
replies.text:
I d/l the Win version of Openshot, though the documentation says it supports VST audio plugin effects, I couldn't get it to work. Maybe this will get implemented/fixed on their next release? I certainly hope so!  
If you have a Windows or Mac machine available to use, I'd highly recommend using Reaper as a viable editing software. It's free and relatively easy to use.  It's primarily used for Audio production but has Video editing capability's and can easily use VST plugins to help clean up the audio and cancel out those room reverberations/acoustics.  
Please feel free to send me a private message if you'd like any help with editing your videos. 
Keep up the GREAT work you've been doing for our world! I've really enjoyed watching and learning through your progress.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I use linux mint sound is an issue on linux
